chunk_summary: Management of neurodisability-associated undernutrition requires individualized nutrition therapy, feeding safety assessments, and integration with India's RBSK and DEIC services to address unique challenges like aspiration risk, prolonged meals, and variable energy needs.
learning_objectives: Understand the impact of neurodisability on nutritional needs and feeding safety.
Learn to assess and modify feeding practices for children with conditions like cerebral palsy.
Identify when to escalate to supplemental tube feeding to prevent complications.
Utilize India's RBSK and DEIC pathways for therapy and assistive services.
key_facts: Feeding safety screen should be conducted at every visit, focusing on choking, coughing, wet voice, recurrent pneumonia, or meals lasting >30 minutes.
Energy needs vary based on tone and mobility; reassess frequently and prioritize protein and micronutrient adequacy.
Use energy-dense home foods like oil/ghee in porridge, nut pastes, and dairy to promote weight gain.
Constipation exacerbates appetite and reflux; treat aggressively to improve feeding outcomes.
Delaying tube feeding escalation can worsen respiratory morbidity and caregiver burnout.
Link to RBSK and DEIC services for therapy and assistive care, ensuring consistent documentation of feeding plans.
reference_quote: Use RBSK and District Early Intervention Centers (DEIC) for therapy and assistive services, and document the feeding plan and aspiration precautions explicitly so multiple providers can deliver consistent care.
document_key_topics: Integration with RBSK and DEIC services
chunk_heading: STUNTING, CHRONIC UNDERNUTRITION AND DETERMINANTS;MANAGEMENT MODIFICATIONS IN NEURODISABILITY-ASSOCIATED UNDERNUTRITION;
